---
title: "colons_check: more examples"
---
<br>

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
    error = TRUE, 
    collapse = FALSE,
    comment = "##"
)
```

```{r setup}
library(saferDev)
```
<br>

### Datasets

```{r}
# Function.
fun1 <- function(){
    base::sum(1, 3)
  }
# Function.
fun2 <- function(){
    sum(1, 3)
  }
```
<br>

### Datasets info

```{r}
fun1 # Function.
fun2 # Function.
```
<br>

### Argument <code>x</code>
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
# Test if all the functions inside fun1 have their associated package written.
# Here everything is fine.
colons_check(x = fun1)
# Here one missing package declaration.
colons_check(x = fun2)
```
<br>

### Argument safer_chek 
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
# Safer checkings are performed before main code running.
colons_check(
    x = fun1, 
    afer_check = TRUE
)
```
<br>

### Argument <code>lib_path</code>
```{r}
# Error returned because the R required packages are not in the "." path.
colons_check(
    x = fun1,  
    lib_path = "."
)
```
<br>

### Argument <code>error_text</code>
```{r}
# Add a text in error messages returned by colons_check().
colons_check(
    x = fun1, 
    lib_path = ".", 
    error_text = " === TEXT ADDED ==="
)
```
<br>

### All the arguments
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
colons_check(
    x = fun1, # function name to test, written without quotes and brackets
    safer_check = TRUE, # Perform some "safer" checks (see https://github.com/safer-r)?
    lib_path = NULL, # absolute pathways of the directories containing the required packages if not in the default directories.
    error_text = "" # add information in error messages returned by the function.
)
```
<br>

### Sophisticated example
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
source("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/safer-r/saferDev/main/dev/other/test.R")
colons_check(
    x = test
)
```
<br>
